clusters.title: Trump announces new effort to escort ships out of Strait of Hormuz
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump says US to begin escorting ships in Strait of Hormuz
bullet_summary: President Trump announced on Sunday that Project Freedom, an effort to escort ships not involved in the war with Iran out of the Strait of Hormuz, will begin Monday
The United States will launch an effort on Monday to "guide" stranded ships from the Iran-gripped Strait of Hormuz, President Donald Trump said, as two ships around the strait reported attacks
US President Donald Trump has announced a naval mission to escort stranded ships through the Strait of Hormuz
The United States is set to begin escorting stranded third-country vessels through the Strait of Hormuz starting Monday morning, US President Donald Trump announced Sunday

items.title: Trump announces new effort to escort ships out of Strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: President Trump announced on Sunday that Project Freedom, an effort to escort ships not involved in the war with Iran out of the Strait of Hormuz, will begin Monday.
items.content: President Trump announced on Sunday that Project Freedom, an effort to escort ships not involved in the war with Iran out of the Strait of Hormuz, will begin Monday.

items.title: Trump says the U.S will 'guide' stranded ships from the Strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: The United States will launch an effort on Monday to "guide" stranded ships from the Iran-gripped Strait of Hormuz, President Donald Trump said, as two ships around the strait reported attacks.
items.content: The United States will launch an effort on Monday to "guide" stranded ships from the Iran-gripped Strait of Hormuz, President Donald Trump said, as two ships around the strait reported attacks.(Image credit: Razieh Poudat/AP)

items.title: Trump says US to begin escorting ships in Strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump has announced a naval mission to escort stranded ships through the Strait of Hormuz.
items.content: US President Donald Trump has announced a naval mission to escort stranded ships through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Trump says US to 'guide' stranded ships through Strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: More than 100 aircraft and 15,000 personnel will be part of the operation due to start on Monday, the US military says.
items.content: More than 100 aircraft and 15,000 personnel will be part of the operation due to start on Monday, the US military says.

items.title: US to ‘guide’ stranded ships out of Strait of Hormuz, says Trump
items.standfirst: President says ‘humanitarian’ effort to begin on Monday as ‘very positive discussions’ continue with Iran
items.content: President says ‘humanitarian’ effort to begin on Monday as ‘very positive discussions’ continue with Iran

items.title: Live: US to escort ships through Strait of Hormuz beginning Monday, Trump says
items.standfirst: The United States is set to begin escorting stranded third-country vessels through the Strait of Hormuz starting Monday morning, US President Donald Trump announced Sunday. Iran quickly pushed back, with a senior official warning that any “American interference” would be seen as a breach of the ceasefire. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
items.content: The United States is set to begin escorting stranded third-country vessels through the Strait of Hormuz starting Monday morning, US President Donald Trump announced Sunday. Iran quickly pushed back, with a senior official warning that any “American interference” would be seen as a breach of the ceasefire. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: Iran war live: Tehran warns Trump’s Hormuz mission will violate ceasefire
items.standfirst: Iran protests as Trump announces 'Project Freedom' to free stranded ships out of the Strait of Hormuz.
items.content: Iran protests as Trump announces 'Project Freedom' to free stranded ships out of the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Trump says US navy will ‘guide’ trapped ships from strait of Hormuz amid ‘very positive’ talks with Iran
items.standfirst: US president’s post follows flurry of mixed signals including concern Tehran had not ‘paid big enough price’Donald Trump has announced that the US will “guide” ships trapped by the Iran war out of the Gulf through the strait of Hormuz on Monday morning, and claimed his representatives were having “very positive” discussions with Iran.Trump wrote on his social media site that the operation, called “Project Freedom”, would be a humanitarian gesture “on behalf of the United States, Middle Eastern Countries but, in particular, the Country of Iran”. Continue reading...
items.content: US president’s post follows flurry of mixed signals including concern Tehran had not ‘paid big enough price’Donald Trump has announced that the US will “guide” ships trapped by the Iran war out of the Gulf through the strait of Hormuz on Monday morning, and claimed his representatives were having “very positive” discussions with Iran.Trump wrote on his social media site that the operation, called “Project Freedom”, would be a humanitarian gesture “on behalf of the United States, Middle Eastern Countries but, in particular, the Country of Iran”. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump says U.S. will begin guiding ships through Strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump said Sunday that the U.S. will guide ships through the Strait of Hormuz, as he has offered little optimism that he will accept a recent proposal from Tehran to end the war
items.content: President Donald Trump said Sunday that the U.S. will guide ships through the Strait of Hormuz, as he has offered little optimism that he will accept a recent proposal from Tehran to end the war

items.title: Trump says US will ‘help free up’ ships stuck in Hormuz Strait
items.standfirst: US president says any interference with operation, which will start on Monday, will 'have to be dealt with forcefully'.
items.content: US president says any interference with operation, which will start on Monday, will 'have to be dealt with forcefully'.

items.title: US President Trump says not likely to accept Iranian proposal
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump said Saturday he was going to review a new Iranian peace proposal but said he doubted it would be acceptable, saying that Tehran had "not yet paid a big enough price". Trump’s comments came after Iran's Tasnim and Fars news agencies said Tehran had submitted a 14-point proposal to mediator Pakistan. France 24 Correspondent Reza Sayah reports from Tehran.
items.content: US President Donald Trump said Saturday he was going to review a new Iranian peace proposal but said he doubted it would be acceptable, saying that Tehran had "not yet paid a big enough price". Trump’s comments came after Iran's Tasnim and Fars news agencies said Tehran had submitted a 14-point proposal to mediator Pakistan. France 24 Correspondent Reza Sayah reports from Tehran.

items.title: Trump says he is reviewing Iran's new offer but doubts it is 'acceptable'
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump said Saturday that he was reviewing a new offer from Iran to end the war, but offered little optimism that the proposal would succeed.“I will soon be reviewing the plan that Iran has just sent to us, but can’t imagine that it would be acceptable in that they have not yet paid a big enough price for what they have done to Humanity, and the World, over the last 47 years,” the president wrote in a Truth Social post
items.content: President Donald Trump said Saturday that he was reviewing a new offer from Iran to end the war, but offered little optimism that the proposal would succeed.“I will soon be reviewing the plan that Iran has just sent to us, but can’t imagine that it would be acceptable in that they have not yet paid a big enough price for what they have done to Humanity, and the World, over the last 47 years,” the president wrote in a Truth Social post

clusters.title: Three dead in suspected virus outbreak on Atlantic cruise ship
clusters.neutral_headline: Three dead in suspected virus outbreak on Atlantic cruise ship
bullet_summary: The World Health Organization said one case of hantavirus infection had been confirmed, with five more suspected cases under investigation
The WHO said an investigation was under way but that at least one case of hantavirus had been confirmed

items.title: Three dead in suspected virus outbreak on Atlantic cruise ship
items.standfirst: The World Health Organization said one case of hantavirus infection had been confirmed, with five more suspected cases under investigation.
items.content: The World Health Organization said one case of hantavirus infection had been confirmed, with five more suspected cases under investigation.

items.title: What is the hantavirus that has been confirmed on an Atlantic cruise ship?
items.standfirst: While rare, the disease can be transmitted to humans through inhalation of airborne particles from dried rodent droppings.
items.content: While rare, the disease can be transmitted to humans through inhalation of airborne particles from dried rodent droppings.

items.title: Suspected hantavirus infections leave 3 dead, several ill on a cruise ship in the Atlantic
items.standfirst: Three passengers have died and three other people are being treated amid a "public health event" involving suspected hantavirus infections on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean, health officials said
items.content: Three passengers have died and three other people are being treated amid a "public health event" involving suspected hantavirus infections on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean, health officials said

items.title: Hantavirus outbreak kills 3 on cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean, WHO says
items.standfirst: Hantaviruses are usually spread by exposure to urine, saliva or feces from infected rodents, such as rats or mice. Hantavirus infections are rare but can cause deadly respiratory infections.
items.content: Hantaviruses are usually spread by exposure to urine, saliva or feces from infected rodents, such as rats or mice. Hantavirus infections are rare but can cause deadly respiratory infections.(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)

items.title: Three passengers dead after suspected hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship
items.standfirst: Dutch husband and wife and third unidentified person reported to have died, with three further people taken illA suspected outbreak of the rare hantavirus infection on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean killed three people – including an elderly married couple – and sickened at least three others, the World Health Organisation (WHO) and South Africa’s health department said on Sunday.The WHO said an investigation was under way but that at least one case of hantavirus had been confirmed. One of the patients was in intensive care in a South African hospital, the UN’s health agency said in a statement to the Associated Press, and the WHO was working with authorities to evacuate two others with symptoms from the ship. Continue reading...
items.content: Dutch husband and wife and third unidentified person reported to have died, with three further people taken illA suspected outbreak of the rare hantavirus infection on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean killed three people – including an elderly married couple – and sickened at least three others, the World Health Organisation (WHO) and South Africa’s health department said on Sunday.The WHO said an investigation was under way but that at least one case of hantavirus had been confirmed. One of the patients was in intensive care in a South African hospital, the UN’s health agency said in a statement to the Associated Press, and the WHO was working with authorities to evacuate two others with symptoms from the ship. Continue reading...

items.title: Three dead in suspected hantavirus outbreak on Atlantic cruise ship
items.standfirst: Three of six passengers ill from a suspected rodent-transmitted virus have died, and one is in intensive care, WHO says.
items.content: Three of six passengers ill from a suspected rodent-transmitted virus have died, and one is in intensive care, WHO says.

items.title: Three dead on Atlantic cruise ship in suspected Hantavirus outbreak, says WHO
items.standfirst: Three people have died aboard a cruise ship crossing the Atlantic after an outbreak that includes a confirmed case of hantavirus, the World Health Organization said Sunday, raising concerns about a rare but potentially severe infection at sea.
items.content: Three people have died aboard a cruise ship crossing the Atlantic after an outbreak that includes a confirmed case of hantavirus, the World Health Organization said Sunday, raising concerns about a rare but potentially severe infection at sea.

clusters.title: Former New York mayor Rudy Giuliani hospitalised in critical condition
clusters.neutral_headline: Rudy Giuliani hospitalised in ‘critical but stable’ condition
bullet_summary: Former New York City Mayor Rudy Giuliani has been hospitalised and is in “critical but stable condition”, his spokesperson Ted Goodman said Sunday, without providing further details
Reported by France24, CBS News, BBC Top Stories and others

items.title: Former New York mayor Rudy Giuliani hospitalised in critical condition
items.standfirst: Former New York City Mayor Rudy Giuliani has been hospitalised and is in “critical but stable condition”, his spokesperson Ted Goodman said Sunday, without providing further details. Giuliani, 81, rose to prominence after the September 11 attacks and later served as an attorney for Donald Trump during efforts to challenge the 2020 election results.
items.content: Former New York City Mayor Rudy Giuliani has been hospitalised and is in “critical but stable condition”, his spokesperson Ted Goodman said Sunday, without providing further details. Giuliani, 81, rose to prominence after the September 11 attacks and later served as an attorney for Donald Trump during efforts to challenge the 2020 election results.

items.title: Rudy Giuliani hospitalized in critical condition, spokesperson says
items.standfirst: There was no immediate reason given for the 81-year-old's hospitalization.
items.content: There was no immediate reason given for the 81-year-old's hospitalization.

items.title: Former New York City mayor Rudy Giuliani in critical condition in hospital
items.standfirst: The long-time champion of Trump and former New York mayor is in hospital, according to his spokesman.
items.content: The long-time champion of Trump and former New York mayor is in hospital, according to his spokesman.

items.title: Rudy Giuliani hospitalised in ‘critical but stable’ condition
items.standfirst: Spokesperson calls former New York City mayor ‘a fighter’ but does not say cause of his hospitalizationRudy Giuliani, former mayor of New York City, has been hospitalised and is in a “critical but stable condition”, his spokesperson said on Sunday evening.Ted Goodman, the spokesperson, posted on social media: “Mayor Giuliani is a fighter who has faced every challenge in his life with unwavering strength, and he’s fighting with that same level of strength as we speak. We do ask that you join us in prayer for America’s Mayor Rudy Giuliani.” Continue reading...
items.content: Spokesperson calls former New York City mayor ‘a fighter’ but does not say cause of his hospitalizationRudy Giuliani, former mayor of New York City, has been hospitalised and is in a “critical but stable condition”, his spokesperson said on Sunday evening.Ted Goodman, the spokesperson, posted on social media: “Mayor Giuliani is a fighter who has faced every challenge in his life with unwavering strength, and he’s fighting with that same level of strength as we speak. We do ask that you join us in prayer for America’s Mayor Rudy Giuliani.” Continue reading...

bullet_summary: official speaking on the condition of anonymity said Sunday that two U
The US, Morocco and other countries participating in the African Lion exercise have launched a search and rescue operation, Africom said

items.title: 2 U.S. service members missing amid training exercise in Morocco
items.standfirst: The service members were participating in African Lion, the largest joint military exercise on the continent.
items.content: The service members were participating in African Lion, the largest joint military exercise on the continent.

items.title: 2 missing U.S. soldiers in Morocco fell off cliff in hiking accident, official says
items.standfirst: A U.S. official speaking on the condition of anonymity said Sunday that two U.S. service members missing in Morocco fell off a cliff into the ocean in what's being described as a hiking accident. They were in the North African country for training exercises. Chris Livesay has more.
items.content: A U.S. official speaking on the condition of anonymity said Sunday that two U.S. service members missing in Morocco fell off a cliff into the ocean in what's being described as a hiking accident. They were in the North African country for training exercises. Chris Livesay has more.

items.title: Two US service members reported missing in Morocco, officials say
items.standfirst: A search and rescue operation is underway for the service members, who are believed to have been involved in an accident, according to authorities.
items.content: A search and rescue operation is underway for the service members, who are believed to have been involved in an accident, according to authorities.

items.title: Two US service members missing after joint military exercises in Morocco
items.standfirst: Two members of the US Armed Forces participating in the African Lion joint military exercises have been reported missing near the city of Tan Tan in southern Morocco, the US Africa Command (AFRICOM) said on Sunday. A US defence official said the soldiers went missing while on a hike after the day's exercises had concluded.
items.content: Two members of the US Armed Forces participating in the African Lion joint military exercises have been reported missing near the city of Tan Tan in southern Morocco, the US Africa Command (AFRICOM) said on Sunday. A US defence official said the soldiers went missing while on a hike after the day's exercises had concluded.

items.title: Two US service members missing after military exercises in Morocco
items.standfirst: Search and rescue operation launched after service members reported missing near south-western city of Tan TanTwo US service members are missing in south-western Morocco after taking part in annual multinational military exercises in the North African country, the United States Africa Command (Africom) said on Sunday.The US, Morocco and other countries participating in the African Lion exercise have launched a search and rescue operation, Africom said. Continue reading...
items.content: Search and rescue operation launched after service members reported missing near south-western city of Tan TanTwo US service members are missing in south-western Morocco after taking part in annual multinational military exercises in the North African country, the United States Africa Command (Africom) said on Sunday.The US, Morocco and other countries participating in the African Lion exercise have launched a search and rescue operation, Africom said. Continue reading...

clusters.title: European and Canadian leaders hold security talks in Yerevan amid uncertainty over US policy
clusters.neutral_headline: Canada to be first non-European nation at EPC summit as Carney seeks allies
bullet_summary: Carney has said he is determined to build a new network of trade and diplomatic alliances after the loss of US markets under Donald Trump
Reported by France24, BBC Top Stories, Guardian World

items.title: European and Canadian leaders hold security talks in Yerevan amid uncertainty over US policy
items.standfirst: European leaders and Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney are meeting in Armenia on Monday for a European Political Community summit focused on security and geopolitical tensions as uncertainty grows over US policy under President Donald Trump. The talks in Yerevan come amid strains over the Iran conflict, the war in Ukraine and shifting transatlantic ties.
items.content: European leaders and Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney are meeting in Armenia on Monday for a European Political Community summit focused on security and geopolitical tensions as uncertainty grows over US policy under President Donald Trump. The talks in Yerevan come amid strains over the Iran conflict, the war in Ukraine and shifting transatlantic ties.

items.title: UK to enter talks to join £78bn EU loan scheme for Ukraine
items.standfirst: Sir Keir Starmer will meet leaders at the European Political Community summit in Armenia on Monday.
items.content: Sir Keir Starmer will meet leaders at the European Political Community summit in Armenia on Monday.

items.title: Canada to be first non-European nation at EPC summit as Carney seeks allies
items.standfirst: PM’s attendance at European Political Community meeting in Yerevan seen as part of effort to build new ties after US ruptureCanada is to become the first non-European country to attend a meeting of the European Political Community when the prime minister, Mark Carney, joins Monday’s summit of the 48-plus nation grouping in Yerevan, Armenia.Carney has said he is determined to build a new network of trade and diplomatic alliances after the loss of US markets under Donald Trump. His presence will also represent a show of western support for Armenia in its efforts to distance itself from Russia at a time when Washington’s approach to Moscow’s opponents, such as Ukraine, is at best ambiguous. Canadian diplomats have rejected suggestions Ottawa might seek EU membership. Continue reading...
items.content: PM’s attendance at European Political Community meeting in Yerevan seen as part of effort to build new ties after US ruptureCanada is to become the first non-European country to attend a meeting of the European Political Community when the prime minister, Mark Carney, joins Monday’s summit of the 48-plus nation grouping in Yerevan, Armenia.Carney has said he is determined to build a new network of trade and diplomatic alliances after the loss of US markets under Donald Trump. His presence will also represent a show of western support for Armenia in its efforts to distance itself from Russia at a time when Washington’s approach to Moscow’s opponents, such as Ukraine, is at best ambiguous. Canadian diplomats have rejected suggestions Ottawa might seek EU membership. Continue reading...

bullet_summary: Local officials said bomb disposal experts had to intervene after a shell was discovered near a road running through the party site

items.title: Illegal ‘free party’ at French military site draws up to 40,000 ravers
items.standfirst: Tens of thousands of partygoers gathered for an illegal “free party” at a military firing range near Bourges.
items.content: Tens of thousands of partygoers gathered for an illegal “free party” at a military firing range near Bourges.

items.title: Illegal party at French military site draws nearly 40,000
items.standfirst: Up to 40,000 people gathered for an illegal rave at a former military site in France. Local officials said bomb disposal experts had to intervene after a shell was discovered near a road running through the party site. Camille Corcoran reports.
items.content: Up to 40,000 people gathered for an illegal rave at a former military site in France. Local officials said bomb disposal experts had to intervene after a shell was discovered near a road running through the party site. Camille Corcoran reports.

clusters.title: Israeli court extends detention of two Gaza flotilla activists amid abuse claims
clusters.neutral_headline: Spain demands release of Gaza flotilla activists ‘held illegally’ by Israel

items.title: Israeli court extends detention of two Gaza flotilla activists amid abuse claims
items.standfirst: An Israeli court on Sunday extended by two days the detention of two foreign activists seized from a Gaza-bound flotilla, as a legal battle intensified over their arrest in international waters and allegations of abuse in custody. A lawyer for one of the activists said the pair were presented with a list of suspected offences that include "assisting the enemy during wartime" and "membership in and providing services to a terrorist organisation".
items.content: An Israeli court on Sunday extended by two days the detention of two foreign activists seized from a Gaza-bound flotilla, as a legal battle intensified over their arrest in international waters and allegations of abuse in custody. A lawyer for one of the activists said the pair were presented with a list of suspected offences that include "assisting the enemy during wartime" and "membership in and providing services to a terrorist organisation".

items.title: Spain demands release of Gaza flotilla activists ‘held illegally’ by Israel
items.standfirst: Israeli court extends detention of two men who were among 175 people intercepted near Crete on ThursdaySpain’s foreign ministry has demanded the immediate release of a Spanish national it said was being “held illegally” by Israel after the interception of a Gaza-bound flotilla, hours after an Israeli court moved to extend his detention by two days.Saif Abu Keshek, who lives in Barcelona, and Thiago Ávila, from Brazil, appeared in court in Ashkelon on Sunday, days after Israeli forces intercepted at least 22 boats from a flotilla that was attempting to break Israel’s maritime blockade of the devastated Palestinian territory to deliver aid. Continue reading...
items.content: Israeli court extends detention of two men who were among 175 people intercepted near Crete on ThursdaySpain’s foreign ministry has demanded the immediate release of a Spanish national it said was being “held illegally” by Israel after the interception of a Gaza-bound flotilla, hours after an Israeli court moved to extend his detention by two days.Saif Abu Keshek, who lives in Barcelona, and Thiago Ávila, from Brazil, appeared in court in Ashkelon on Sunday, days after Israeli forces intercepted at least 22 boats from a flotilla that was attempting to break Israel’s maritime blockade of the devastated Palestinian territory to deliver aid. Continue reading...

items.title: Two people dead after explosion at house in Bristol
items.standfirst: Avon and Somerset police declare major incident and say cause is being treated as suspiciousTwo people have died and three people, including a child, have been injured after an explosion at a house in Bristol that police have said is “suspicious”.Avon and Somerset police were called to the house in Sterncourt Road at about 6.17am on Sunday for a “domestic-related incident”, Supt Matt Ebbs said. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Drugmaker asks Supreme Court to halt ruling blocking online access to mifepristone

bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, BBC World

items.title: Drugmaker asks Supreme Court to halt ruling blocking online access to mifepristone
items.standfirst: A maker of the widely used abortion pill mifepristone​ asked the Supreme Court on Saturday to block an appellate court ruling that cut off mail-order access to the drug just a day earlier.
items.content: A maker of the widely used abortion pill mifepristone​ asked the Supreme Court on Saturday to block an appellate court ruling that cut off mail-order access to the drug just a day earlier.

items.title: US Supreme Court asked to restore abortion pill access
items.standfirst: A lower court has limited mail-order access to the abortion pill mifepristone, which the drug's maker says will cause "irreparable harm".
items.content: A lower court has limited mail-order access to the abortion pill mifepristone, which the drug's maker says will cause "irreparable harm".
